science.title: Scientists create supercharged vitamin K that helps the brain heal itself
science.summary: Scientists in Japan have created powerful new vitamin K-based compounds that may help the brain regenerate lost neurons — a breakthrough that could one day change how diseases like Alzheimer’s and Parkinson’s are treated. science.title: Scottish wrens may be evolving into new species through island gigantism
science.summary: Tiny birds on remote Scottish islands are undergoing a dramatic evolutionary transformation. Scientists studying four isolated populations of British Wrens discovered that some island birds have grown astonishingly large — with the biggest St Kilda Wrens weighing more than twice as much as the smallest mainland birds.

science.title: 100-million-year-old bug had crab-like claws unlike any known insect
science.summary: Deep inside 100-million-year-old amber from Myanmar, scientists uncovered a bizarre ancient bug with clawed front legs that look more like a crab’s pincers than anything seen in modern insects. The discovery is so unusual that researchers say these crab-like “chelae” evolved independently in this lineage, making it onl

science.title: MIT scientists discover amino acid that helps the gut heal itself
science.summary: MIT scientists have identified cysteine — an amino acid found in foods like meat, dairy, beans, and nuts — as a potent trigger for intestinal repair. In mice, a cysteine-rich diet activated immune cells that released healing signals, helping stem cells rebuild damaged intestinal tissue after radiation exposure. Researc

science.title: Magnon Breakthrough Could Shrink Quantum Computers to the Size of a Penny
science.summary: Physicists at the University of Vienna have discovered magnons with lifespans that are one hundred times longer. Magnons are tiny waves of magnetization that move through solid magnetic materials, similar to ripples spreading across water after a stone falls in. tech.title: Amazon Thinks the Future of Data Centers Depends on a Technical Problem It Just Solved
tech.summary: The tech giant says a breakthrough in data center networking has dramatically accelerated the flow of information through its massive cloud infrastructure.

tech.title: Waymo’s newest robotaxi is Chinese-made, built to make money, and now accepting riders
tech.summary: The launch of the Ojai minivan robotaxi comes after years of development and testing, but arrives amid a challenging time for Waymo.

tech.title: Microsoft 365 Copilot gets a speed boost and cleaner design
tech.summary: Microsoft is launching a revamped version of Microsoft 365 Copilot, offering a cleaner design that the company claims loads twice as fast. tech.title: The $6 Billion Chinese Startup Trying to Build Hands for Every Robot
tech.summary: LinkerBot makes dexterous robotic hands for as little as $600. It wants to become the standard for humanoids and automated factories—and eventually replace human labor altogether.

tech.title: Here Comes Ojai, Waymo’s New Chinese-Made Robotaxi
tech.summary: The pale-blue Ojai vehicles will start picking up members of the public in California and Arizona in the next few weeks.

medicine.title: New drug could finally stop deadly fatty liver disease
medicine.summary: Scientists at UC San Diego have unveiled a potentially game-changing treatment for MASH, a severe fatty liver disease affecting millions worldwide. medicine.title: Ocrelizumab cuts disability progression in primary progressive MS trial

medicine.title: Scientists discover the nutrient that can supercharge cellular energy
medicine.summary: Researchers discovered that leucine, a nutrient found in protein-rich foods, can supercharge mitochondria by protecting crucial energy-producing proteins inside cells. The breakthrough uncovers a powerful new link between diet and cellular energy — with possible implications for cancer and metabolic disease treatments.

medicine.title: Common anemia medication shows unexpected potential in cancer treatment
medicine.summary: Common medications used to treat anemia may also slow down cancer cell growth, according to new research from Finland. Researchers from the University of Oulu and the University of Eastern Finland discovered that these drugs affect cell metabolism and growth in ways previously unknown. The findings, published in Redox

mathematics.title: MIT affiliates win AI for Math grants to accelerate mathematical discovery
mathematics.summary: Department of Mathematics researchers David Roe and Andrew Sutherland seek to advance automated theorem proving; four additional MIT alumni also awarded.

mathematics.title: Two Researchers Are Rebuilding Mathematics From the Ground Up
mathematics.summary: By replacing the most fundamental concept in topology, Peter Scholze and Dustin Clausen are taking the first step in a far bigger program to understand why numbers behave the way they do. The post Two Researchers Are Rebuilding Mathematics From the Ground Up first appeared on Quanta Magazine

mathematics.title: How Alexander Grothendieck Revolutionized 20th-Century Mathematics

mathematics.title: What Do Gödel’s Incompleteness Theorems Truly Mean?

mathematics.title: How Unknowable Math Can Help Hide Secrets

space.summary: NASA’s Nancy Grace Roman Space Telescope is poised to make a major leap in the hunt for worlds outside our solar system, known as exoplanets. space.title: Companies like SpaceX want electromagnetic catapults on the moon. Could they be used as weapons?
space.summary: A new report stresses the strategic and security implications of placing mass drivers on the moon by arguing that these launchers could serve as valuable first strike weapons systems.

space.title: NASA is hatching a 'fast-paced plan' to boost this space telescope. But first, they'll have to find it
space.summary: NASA is working hard to predict where in Earth orbit its Swift space telescope will be this fall, so that a private spacecraft can meet up with the observatory and boost its altitude.

space.title: What's next for SpaceX's Starship V3 megarocket after its historic debut flight?
space.summary: SpaceX's "V3" Starship megarocket did pretty well on its debut flight last week. What's next for the powerful next-gen vehicle?

astronomy.title: JWST Studies a Dark and Airless Super-Earth
astronomy.summary: There's a planet out there called LHS 3844 b, orbiting a star about 48 light-years away. The Transiting Exoplanet Survey Satellite (TESS) found it in 2018 when the planet transited across the face of its star. The James Webb Space Telescope zxeroed in on the planet and found it to be a barren, rocky place with no atmos

astronomy.title: Close Encounter: Jupiter and Venus
astronomy.summary: The two brightest planets in our sky will be less than 2 degrees apart on June 9th at sunset. The post Close Encounter: Jupiter and Venus appeared first on Sky & Telescope.

astronomy.title: Lost in the Star Clouds — A Milky Way Odyssey
astronomy.summary: I share my "discovery" of a new Milky Way star cloud that's been staring at me for ages. The post Lost in the Star Clouds — A Milky Way Odyssey appeared first on Sky & Telescope.

astronomy.title: This Week's Sky at a Glance, May 22 – 31
astronomy.summary: Venus and Jupiter — the Evening Star and the False Evening Star — draw closer together in the western twilight and point down to Mercury. The waxing Moon shows off its rich telescopic detail. The post This Week's Sky at a Glance, May 22 – 31 appeared first on Sky & Telescope.

astronomy.title: Earthly Hors d'oeuvres For Hungry Red Dwarfs
astronomy.summary: We know that stars can engulf planets because stars that swell up to become red giants overwhelm any close-in planets. The Sun will do this to Venus, Mercury, and possibly Earth in a few billion years. But research shows that it can happen when low-mass stars first enter the main sequence. Lithium gives it away.

ai.summary: Parameter Golf brought together 1,000+ participants and 2,000+ submissions to explore AI-assisted machine learning research, coding agents, quantization, and novel model design under strict constraints.
